businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends well beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin reached 20.1%, a two-year high. FSD transitioned to a $99/month subscription model for recurring revenue. April 2026 brings Cybercab mass production and Roadster unveiling as key growth catalysts.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg ~23x and auto sector avg ~12x. Declining with price but still elevated

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026E EPS of $2.08. Over 4x the growth stock avg of 30-40x. Prices in explosive robotaxi and energy growth

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: Due April 2 (Wed). Seeking Alpha says 370,000+ possible, but price cuts may hurt margins

keyMetrics.name: 2026 Delivery Guidance
keyMetrics.interpretation: Downward revision reflects protest impact and weak European sales

keyMetrics.name: WTI Oil Price
keyMetrics.interpretation: Oil above $100 for first time since July 2022. Dual effect: EV demand boost vs consumer spending pressure

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Approaching oversold threshold of 30. Four consecutive down days. Technical bounce possible

growthDrivers.title: $100 Oil Era Could Accelerate EV Adoption
growthDrivers.description: WTI above $100 pushes US gasoline toward $4/gallon. CarGurus data shows $4 is the psychological tipping point where EV search interest surges. Tesla's 50%+ US EV market share makes it the primary beneficiary

growthDrivers.title: Tesla Holds 50%+ Share as EV Market Drops 28% (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Despite a 28% decline in the overall EV market, Tesla expanded its market share above 50%. Brand dominance and Supercharger infrastructure prove most valuable during downturns

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Mass Production + Roadster Unveiling in April
growthDrivers.description: Steerless Cybercab robotaxi production and next-gen Roadster reveal both scheduled for April. Two major catalysts concentrated in one month

growthDrivers.title: Optimus3 Robot Demonstration, Summer Production Start (3/25)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla revealed Optimus3 humanoid robot gearbox and precision hand designs. Targeting 1M unit production rate by year-end 2026. Trefis values the robot business at $2-3T

growthDrivers.title: Terafab $25B AI Chip Factory, Wedbush Calls It 'Game Changer'
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush rates Terafab semiconductor factory plans as a 'game changer,' maintaining $600 target. AI5 chip small-scale production begins late 2026, full production in 2027

growthDrivers.title: NHTSA Escalates FSD Investigation (3/19)
growthDrivers.description: NHTSA escalated its probe into FSD performance in low-visibility conditions (fog, dust). Trefis warns this could 'shatter the $1 trillion AI thesis'

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share, 8.4B FSD miles of data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip vertical integration

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD driving miles. Scale of autonomous driving training data that competitors cannot replicate in the short term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and other competitors now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantages and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand awareness and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China's #1 EV maker, 100%+ growth in Europe. Price competitiveness advantage, rapidly closing the gap

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi service in SF and Las Vegas. Pilot operations in Austin and Miami. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Leads Tesla in autonomous driving commercialization. 200K+ paid rides per week. Expanding robotaxi services

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Expanding global EV share with Ioniq, EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Seeking Alpha: 'Tesla May Beat Deliveries, But This Could Hurt the Bottom Line'
recentNews.significance: Seeking Alpha projects 370,000+ deliveries beating the 365,645 consensus, but warns that price cuts and incentives could erode margins. A delivery beat may not translate to good news for profitability

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Is Tesla Stock Still Overvalued?' EV Pricing Pressure Analysis
recentNews.significance: Intensifying EV price competition coupled with skepticism about AI robotaxi pivot. Stock slid to $355.28, marking four consecutive down days

recentNews.headline: Gary Black: 'Tesla Has Never Lived Up to the Hype,' Q1 Deliveries D-2
recentNews.significance: Prominent Tesla bull Gary Black warns about unmet expectations. Consensus 365,645 units, only +8.6% vs worst quarter in 2025

recentNews.headline: Oil WTI Breaks $100 as Houthi Attacks Expand War Front
recentNews.significance: Houthi missile strikes on Israel expand the conflict. Oil stays above $100. US gasoline approaching $4/gallon creates dual EV demand boost and consumer spending pressure

recentNews.headline: Tesla Cuts 2026 Delivery Guidance from 1.75M to 1.69M
recentNews.significance: ARK trimmed positions, TipRanks reports guidance cut. Protest impact and weak European sales cited as causes

analystOpinions.notableComment: Seeking Alpha: Delivery beat possible (370k+) but margin erosion is 'the real risk.' HSBC $119 vs Wedbush $600. April 2 deliveries D-2

risks.title: Q1 Deliveries D-2 (April 2), Margin Erosion Risk
risks.description: Seeking Alpha projects 370,000+ deliveries but warns price cuts could erode margins. A delivery surprise paired with margin deterioration creates an uncertain stock direction

risks.title: EV Pricing Pressure + AI Pivot Skepticism
risks.description: Motley Fool analyzes ongoing 'EV pricing pressure and AI robotaxi pivot skepticism.' Four consecutive down days to $355.28, down 1.81% from Friday

risks.title: Below Both 50-Day and 200-Day SMAs, RSI 31 Oversold
risks.description: Trading below 50-day SMA ($387) and 200-day SMA ($400) with widening gaps. RSI at 31 entering oversold territory

risks.title: HSBC $119 Target + ARK Selling Simultaneously
risks.description: Both the most prominent bull (ARK) and bear (HSBC) showing negative moves in an unprecedented dual signal

risks.title: FSD Regulatory and Safety Risk Escalation
risks.description: NHTSA probe escalated. Camera-only system under regulatory scrutiny. Trefis warns the '$1 trillion AI thesis could shatter'

risks.title: Extreme Valuation Risk
risks.description: TTM P/E at 329x is 14x the S&P 500 average of 23x. Delivery guidance cut and pricing competition make valuation justification increasingly difficult

buyReasons.title: Q1 Delivery Surprise Potential at 370,000+
buyReasons.rationale: Seeking Alpha projects deliveries above 365,645 consensus. A beat could catalyze a bounce from oversold RSI 31 levels

buyReasons.title: $100 Oil Era Makes Tesla the Prime EV Beneficiary
buyReasons.rationale: US gasoline approaching $4/gallon triggers EV search surge. Tesla's 50%+ EV market share positions it at the center of demand shift

buyReasons.title: April Catalyst Concentration (4/2 Deliveries, Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 Earnings)
buyReasons.rationale: Multiple catalysts concentrated in April create potential for momentum reversal

buyReasons.title: RSI 31 Oversold, Technical Bounce Imminent
buyReasons.rationale: RSI approaching 30 oversold threshold. Down 29% from 52-week high. Four consecutive down days suggest bounce potential

overallOpinion: Q1 deliveries are two days away (April 2). Seeking Alpha projects 370,000+ units beating the 365,645 consensus, but warns price cuts could erode margins. A delivery surprise and margin deterioration could arrive simultaneously, a double-edged sword

EV pricing pressure and AI pivot skepticism drove four consecutive down days to $355.28, now 29% below the 52-week high. Motley Fool asks 'Is Tesla still overvalued?' HSBC's $119 to Wedbush's $600 target spread shows extreme polarization

RSI 31 oversold. Below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400) SMAs. April catalysts (Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 earnings) offer reversal potential, but April 2 deliveries come first. Hold, decide after delivery data

investmentVerdict.summary: Seeking Alpha sees Q1 delivery upside (370k+) but warns margin erosion may follow. RSI 31 oversold with April catalysts concentrated, but EV pricing wars and AI skepticism persist. Waiting for April 2 delivery data before acting is the rational approach

sources.name: Seeking Alpha - Q1 Delivery Beat Possible, Margin Risk
sources.description: 370,000+ delivery projection but price cuts may erode margins

sources.description: EV pricing pressure and AI pivot skepticism analysis

sources.name: Stocktwits - Gary Black Q1 Delivery Warning
sources.description: 'Never lived up to the hype,' 365,645 consensus

sources.name: CNBC - Houthi Missiles, Oil $100+
sources.description: Houthi missile strikes on Israel, oil continues rising

sources.name: TipRanks - ARK Selling, Guidance Cut
sources.description: Delivery outlook cut from 1.75M to 1.69M, ARK trimming

sources.name: Primary Ignition - Q1 Delivery Benchmark
sources.description: Analysis of April 2 delivery benchmark significance

witty.hook: Beating deliveries is good, right? For Tesla, it's complicated
witty.text:
$TSLA $355.28 (-1.81%)

Seeking Alpha: 370k+ deliveries possible.
Sounds great?
They slashed prices to get there.
Margins are melting.

Hold. Wednesday tells us everything.
